ad_copy: 22 years ago, a woman in Rhode Island asked: what if jewelry meant something? It still does.
brand_visual_direction: Where the heritage variation foregrounds craft and the eco variation foregrounds material, this variation foregrounds people. The emotional weight lives in faces — real women, real reactions, the private moment of someone reading words on a card and feeling something true. The grade is warmer and softer than both prior variations — closer to skin, closer to the interior life of a person. Shallow depth of field keeps faces in intimate proximity. The product is genuinely incidental: these women are the subject from beginning to end. The craft and founding story are present but compressed in service of the emotional arc.
generation_prompt: Cinematic emotional portrait film, 16:9, warm and skin-forward color grade — shallow depth of field, faces close, unhurried. Opens on an extreme close-up of copper wire bending between bare hands — total silence, faint metallic whisper. Camera pulls back to reveal a craftsperson in a Cranston workshop, morning window light soft and warm, Attitude of Gratitude bangle worn naturally. Cut to brick exterior, text overlay appears. A woman reads a meaning card alone — the camera stays with her face, every micro-expression held. Overhead of bangles and meaning cards. The montage of five wrists in motion is now more face-inclusive — we see more of the women, not just their wrists: a woman laughing at her kitchen counter, a woman in a car watching the landscape pass, a woman in a garden kneeling, a woman at a desk pausing and looking inward, a woman lifting a morning coffee — each caught mid-truth, bangles incidental. Return to workshop: Glass Butterfly bangle held to window, light scatters. Then three intimate close-ups — three women's faces and wrists, all deeply private. One glances at her Make Your Own Luck Ladybug bangle with a quiet smile. Ends on dark recycled metal surface, Alex and Ani logo in gold, 'Est. 2004.' Sustained silence. Inspired by intimate documentary portraiture, Humans of New York in film form, and the emotional directness of Dove's Real Beauty campaign.

scenes.camera_angle: Eye-level, very close — as intimate as a hand can be
scenes.camera_movement: Static. Not a breath of movement.
scenes.framing: Wire and hands fill the frame. The intimacy of hands — familiar, human, specific.
scenes.key_light: Warm window light from frame-right — slightly softer than the heritage variation
scenes.light_quality: Warm, diffused, skin-forward — the same quality that will light faces in this variation
scenes.light_mood: Intimate. The silence of a person working alone.
scenes.subject: Bare hands bending a copper wire — the humanness of the hands is as important as the wire
scenes.action: Slow, deliberate bend. The hands are as much the subject as the wire — knuckles, fingerprints, the specific way this person holds metal.
scenes.expression: Hands only. But hands can express.
scenes.depth_of_field: Extremely shallow — wire and hands sharp, worktable a warm blur
scenes.color_palette: Skin tone, copper amber, warm cream
scenes.color_grade: Warmer than both other variations — pulled toward skin tone as the unifying color value of this film
scenes.music: None. Complete silence except faint metallic wire sound.

scenes.shot_type: Wide pull-back; cut to exterior; cut returns to craftsperson's face
scenes.camera_angle: Pull-back from Scene 1. Exterior: low angle. Return to craftsperson: medium close-up on face.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow pull-back. Static exterior. Medium close-up on face: static with micro-drift, handheld quality.
scenes.framing: Craftsperson at worktable — then face in medium close-up, warm morning light. Exterior: building in frame, text overlay.
scenes.key_light: Warm morning window light — wraps slightly around the craftsperson's face
scenes.light_quality: Soft, directional — strong enough to create depth but warm enough to feel intimate
scenes.light_mood: A person in their work. The quiet satisfaction of making.
scenes.subject: Craftsperson at worktable (wide); craftsperson's face (close); brick exterior
scenes.action: Craftsperson works. The additional face cut: they pause for a moment — not posed, just the natural pause of a person thinking. Attitude of Gratitude bangle visible on wrist throughout.
scenes.expression: In the face cut: absorbed, thoughtful. Not aware of the camera.
scenes.background: Workshop interior warm. Exterior: Cranston brick.
scenes.depth_of_field: Wide pull-back: medium. Face close-up: shallow — face sharp, workshop falls away.
scenes.color_palette: Warm skin, amber workshop, faded brick
scenes.color_grade: Warmest version of this scene across all five variations — face-forward, skin tone is the key value
scenes.voiceover: There was a question she kept asking.
scenes.text_overlay: Cranston, Rhode Island. 2004. — lower third on exterior cut. Small serif, warm gold.

scenes.shot_type: Extended medium close-up on woman's face — held longer than other variations; brief overhead cut
scenes.camera_angle: Face: eye-level, intimate — closer than comfortable for a standard medium shot. Overhead: directly above worktable.
scenes.camera_movement: Face: handheld micro-drift — feels like the camera is breathing. Overhead: static.
scenes.framing: Face fills more of the frame than in other variations. The reaction is the content.
scenes.key_light: Soft window light from one side — wraps around the face, no harsh shadows
scenes.light_quality: The warmest, softest light in any variation — this is a film about faces, and this face is the center
scenes.light_mood: Interior. Private. The weight of recognition — when something you've felt but couldn't name gets named for you.
scenes.subject: A woman reading a meaning card — her face holds the story. Brief overhead of bangles and meaning cards.
scenes.action: She reads. The camera stays with her for an extended hold. We see her process the words. A small, private reaction — not performed, not dramatic. The overhead is brief — a breath of context.
scenes.expression: This is the most important performance note of the entire film: do not direct this. Let the talent read the card genuinely. What we need is the micro-expression of someone feeling something real.
scenes.depth_of_field: Extremely shallow on face — only the eyes and the slight forward plane of the face are sharp
scenes.color_palette: Skin tones, warm ivory, soft background warm blur
scenes.color_grade: Skin-forward warmth. Slight lift in highlights to keep the face glowing without losing depth.
scenes.voiceover: What if a piece of jewelry could carry something real? Not just gold. Not just silver. Something you'd actually feel.
scenes.music: Acoustic guitar — single melodic phrase, warm and close.

scenes.shot_type: Montage — five women in motion, more face-inclusive than wrist-only
scenes.camera_angle: Varied — more face-and-wrist framing than wrist-only. Some cuts include the woman's face in frame.
scenes.camera_movement: Loose handheld throughout — follows action naturally. Each cut feels observational, caught.
scenes.framing: Some shots wider to include faces — the women are the subject. Bangles visible but not the framing device.
scenes.key_light: Varied per location — kitchen window, car sidelight, garden overcast, desk lamp, café morning light
scenes.light_quality: Each location's own quality — the unifying thread is warmth and intimacy
scenes.light_mood: Alive, specific, plural. Five different lives, five different moments of living.
scenes.subject: Five women in motion — faces and wrists. Token of Love and Glass Butterfly bangles visible in the wrist moments.
scenes.action: A woman laughing at something off-screen in her kitchen. A woman in a car, hand out the window, watching landscape pass. A woman kneeling in a garden, looking at something close to the ground. A woman at a desk, pausing, looking inward. A woman lifting her morning coffee, eyes elsewhere.
scenes.expression: Each woman in her own moment. No camera awareness. The camera observes.
scenes.background: Kitchen warm, car window blur, garden green, desk neutral, café morning
scenes.depth_of_field: Shallow to medium — intimate proximity throughout
scenes.color_palette: Warm kitchen amber, car-window cool, garden green-gold, soft desk neutral, café cream
scenes.color_grade: Skin-forward warmth applied consistently — faces always the warmest, most luminous element in each frame
scenes.music: Second guitar layer enters softly — counter-melody, warm.

scenes.shot_type: Medium close-up — hand and face holding bangle to window; hold on light scatter
scenes.camera_angle: Slightly low — includes the craftsperson's face in soft background as they hold bangle to window
scenes.camera_movement: Very slow push toward light scatter on worktable — the craftsperson's face remains in soft background focus
scenes.framing: Hand and bangle upper frame, light scatter lower. Craftsperson's face visible in the background — soft, present.
scenes.key_light: Morning window light backlighting the bangle — hard through the glass beads
scenes.light_quality: Prismatic through the bangle beads — point-source scatter across wood surface
scenes.light_mood: Wonder. The face in the background holds the same feeling as the light on the table.
scenes.subject: Craftsperson's hand holding Glass Butterfly bangle to window — face visible in soft background
scenes.action: Bangle raised to window. Light scatters. Camera holds on the light for 2 full seconds. The craftsperson's expression in background: quiet wonder, absorbed.
scenes.expression: The face in background is soft but present — the expression is wonder, unhurried.
scenes.background: Window: overexposed white. Worktable: warm amber. Craftsperson face: soft focus, warm.
scenes.depth_of_field: Split — bangle slightly soft, light scatter sharp, face in background very soft
scenes.color_palette: Overexposed white window, warm amber wood, scattered light points, skin tone in background
scenes.color_grade: Warmest frame in the film except Scene 3 — the face in background keeps the skin-tone value central
scenes.voiceover: She just wanted to make something that meant something.

scenes.shot_type: Three intimate portraits — face and wrist together
scenes.camera_angle: Each from a different angle and framing — one face-dominant, one wrist-dominant with face in background, one face and wrist equal
scenes.camera_movement: First: static. Second: very slow, subtle drift. Third: static, slow fade begins.
scenes.framing: This variation's key distinction: we see faces alongside wrists. The emotion is carried by the people, not the jewelry.
scenes.key_light: Soft, varied — each woman in her own light environment
scenes.light_mood: Settled, present. The feeling of wearing something that means something, privately.
scenes.subject: Three women, three ages — faces and wrists. Make Your Own Luck Ladybug bangle visible on one woman's wrist as she glances at it with a quiet, private expression.
scenes.action: First woman: face in frame as she glances at her wrist — we see the glance land. Second: adjusts a bangle, face partially in frame. Third: holds wrist in her lap, face tilted slightly down, still.
scenes.expression: First: a quiet private smile — the smallest possible, not performed. Second: habitual, absent. Third: still, interior.
scenes.background: Soft warm domestic and personal spaces
scenes.depth_of_field: Shallow — faces sharp, backgrounds very soft
scenes.color_palette: Skin tones, warm gold, silver, ambient domestic warmth
scenes.color_grade: Warmest of all the close-up sequences — this is the emotional resolution of the film
scenes.music: Guitar fades to single held note.

scenes.shot_type: Static brand card on textured recycled metal surface
scenes.framing: Logo centered. Text below. Surface fills frame.
scenes.light_quality: Absorbed, flat — the texture is the subject
scenes.light_mood: The long exhale after an emotional journey. Quiet, complete.
scenes.subject: Alex and Ani logo in warm gold. 'Est. 2004.' beneath. Dark textured recycled metal surface.
scenes.action: Nothing moves. Three-second hold in silence.
scenes.background: Recycled copper surface — deep, warm, physical
scenes.color_palette: Deep warm black, copper undertones, warm gold
scenes.color_grade: The sudden contrast from the warmth of Scene 6 faces to the cool-dark of this card is intentional — a held breath after something felt
scenes.text_overlay: Alex and Ani logo — centered, warm gold. 'Est. 2004.' — small, below. No animation.

style_references: Humans of New York — in film form. The truth of an unperformed face.
Dove Real Beauty campaign — real women, real expressions, radical non-performance
Richard Linklater's face-forward cinematography — the face held in close-up long enough to become real
Sally Mann portraiture — intimate, unguarded, honest
Terrence Malick's observational style — camera as witness, not director
negative_prompts: Any smile or expression directed at the camera — all facial expressions must be private and unperformed
Wrist-only shots that exclude the face — this variation requires the human face to anchor every key scene
Over-lit, studio-quality skin with no natural variation — texture, shadow, and imperfection are the authenticity markers
Rapid cuts or montage pacing that does not allow emotional expression to land — faces require time to read
Morphing facial features, uncanny skin textures, or AI-artifact distortion in face close-ups — the most critical quality control point in this variation
Product presented or held toward camera in any shot — the emotional authenticity is undone by any commercial framing instinct
Generic casting — faces should read as specific, lived-in, distinct individuals across age ranges and backgrounds
post_production_notes: Edit rhythm: This variation breathes the longest. The face holds in Scene 3 should run at least 6 seconds without a cut. Resist every editorial instinct to trim it. The discomfort of holding on a face is the point.

Color grade: Skin tone is the primary value — grade to the face, not to the environment. Warm, lifted midtones that honor natural skin without adding artificial glow. No beauty-commercial skin treatment.

Casting and performance: This variation lives or dies by the quality of the unperformed reactions. All talent in Scene 3 and Scene 6 should be given actual Alex and Ani meaning cards to read genuinely, not asked to perform reading them.

Scene 6 micro-expression direction: The quiet private glance at the bangle should be directed as 'check the time on a watch you've worn for five years' — habitual, not precious. The private smile should be the smallest possible expression that reads on camera.
Sound design: Ambient sound beneath the music in Scenes 4 and 5 — real-world presence keeps the emotional weight grounded. No artificial silence except in Scene 1 and Scene 7.

Music mix: The warmest, most intimate guitar tone of all five variations — closer-mic'd, slight room resonance, not a produced studio sound. The guitar should feel like it is in the same room as the viewer.

Scene 5 face in background: The craftsperson's expression in the soft background of the window-light shot must be legible without being the subject — light the background face carefully to retain emotional presence without stealing focus from the light scatter.

Final card transition: The cut from Scene 6's warmth to the dark brand card should be a clean cut, not a dissolve — the contrast is intentional and abrupt. The silence begins on the first frame of Scene 7, not after a brief fade.

scene_2.treatment: Worn naturally on craftsperson's wrist — visible in wide shot and in the close-up face moment.

scene_3.treatment: Environmental overhead. The face shot is the priority — bangles are context, not subject.

scene_4.product: Token of Love Charm Bangle, Glass Butterfly Beaded Charm Bangle
scene_4.treatment: Visible on wrists within the face-and-wrist framing of the montage. Women's expressions and actions are primary.

scene_5.treatment: Held to window — craftsperson's face in soft background adds human weight to the light scatter moment.

scene_6.product: Make Your Own Luck Ladybug Charm Bangle
scene_6.treatment: Visible as the first woman glances at her wrist — her quiet expression is the product benefit, not the bangle itself.

sustainability_notes: In this variation, sustainability is embedded only through the brand card texture and the implicit argument of the 22-year founding story — the idea that jewelry you feel something about doesn't get thrown away. No explicit eco copy. The emotional connection argument is the anti-fast-fashion argument.
